# Word-saved style samples

Hand-crafted Word 365 documents used as the source of authority for
`_BUILTIN_STYLES` defaults in `docx_plus/styles/modify.py`. They are
not generated by `build_fixtures.py`; they are committed binary
artifacts produced by a human applying each latent style to a paragraph
in Microsoft Word and saving the file.

## Why these exist

OOXML's `latentStyles` block declares ~280 styles but ships no defaults
— the actual rPr/pPr definitions only land in `styles.xml` once a style
is applied to content and the document is saved. Reconstructing Word's
defaults from the ECMA-376 spec is error-prone (the spec defines the
schema, not the visual defaults Word 365 uses today). Extracting them
from a real Word-saved doc is the only reliable source.

When `_BUILTIN_STYLES` was expanded from 22 → 107 entries on
2026-05-15, the latent two thirds (TOC*, footnote/endnote/comment
family, BodyText, MacroText, BlockText, PlainText, etc.) were filled
from these two samples on 2026-05-19. The samples also flushed out a
real bug: Word writes `TableofFigures` / `TableofAuthorities` with a
lowercase `o`, but the table had been keyed under `TableOfFigures` /
`TableOfAuthorities` (capital O) and would never have resolved in a
real document.

## How they were produced

1. Open a blank Word 365 document
2. Press **Ctrl+Shift+S** to open the Apply Styles dialog
3. For each target latent style, type the style name and Enter to apply
   it to the current paragraph (Word materialises the definition into
   `styles.xml` on save). Latent styles include `Plain Text`, `HTML
   Preformatted`, `Block Text`, `toc 1`–`toc 9`, `Footnote Text`,
   `Endnote Text`, `Comment Text`, `Comment Subject`, `Balloon Text`,
   `Header`, `Footer`, `Body Text`, `Body Text 2`, `Body Text 3`,
   `Macro Text`, `Index 1`, `Index Heading`, `Table of Figures`,
   `Table of Authorities`, `toa heading`, `Intense Quote`, etc.
4. Save as `.docx`

## Sample contents

| File | Style count | Notable coverage |
|---|---:|---|
| `sample-1.docx` | 60 | First pass — TOC1/2, Footnote/Endnote/Comment family (incl. `CommentSubject` + `CommentSubjectChar`), HTMLPreformatted, PlainText, BlockText, Index1, TableofFigures/Authorities, Caption, IntenseQuote, NormalIndent, NoSpacing |
| `sample-2.docx` | 81 | Second pass — adds Header/Footer (+Char), all of TOC3–TOC9, BodyText/2/3 (+Char), MacroText/Char, BalloonText/Char, IndexHeading. Does not include CommentSubject |

The two together cover all 47 originally-targeted latent entries plus
20-odd surrounding styles. Defaults extracted from these samples are
documented per-tier in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §5`.

## How to use them

To re-extract a style's authoritative defaults (e.g. when adding a new
entry to `_BUILTIN_STYLES`):

```python
import zipfile
from lxml import etree

with zipfile.ZipFile("tests/fixtures/word_samples/sample-2.docx") as z:
    root = etree.fromstring(z.read("word/styles.xml"))

NS_W = "http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main"
for st in root.findall(f"{{{NS_W}}}style"):
    if st.get(f"{{{NS_W}}}styleId") == "TargetStyleId":
        print(etree.tostring(st, pretty_print=True).decode())
```

Style ids in Word's output don't always follow obvious capitalisation
(`TableofFigures`, not `TableOfFigures` — note the lowercase `o`). When
in doubt, list every materialised style id first and grep.

## When to regenerate

These samples capture Word 365's defaults as of 2026-05-19. They should
be regenerated if:

- A new Word version ships meaningfully different defaults (Microsoft
  occasionally tweaks heading colors and theme fonts between releases)
- A future `_BUILTIN_STYLES` expansion adds styles not covered here

Regeneration is manual: open in Word, apply the new style via Ctrl+
Shift+S, save. The samples are not auto-generated; preserving the
provenance of "Microsoft Word itself wrote this" is the whole point.
